sql.schema_migration:
-- Schema migration: add neighborhood, address, hours to donation_links
-- Run once before upsert if columns do not exist.
ALTER TABLE donation_links
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS neighborhood TEXT,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS address TEXT,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS hours TEXT;

donation_links.title: The Salvation Army – Kona Low Storm Disaster Relief 2026
donation_links.organization: The Salvation Army, Hawaiʻi and Pacific Islands Division
donation_links.description: Dedicated Kona low 2026 disaster relief campaign confirmed as the canonical Kona Low-specific campaign. Salvation Army confirmed active statewide operations at March 21 city press conference with Honolulu Mayor Blangiardi. Financial assistance application process announced for flood-impacted residents. Campaign 728881 (‘Hope When Disaster Strikes’ general fund) has been superseded by this event-specific record.

donation_links.title: Hawaiʻi Community Foundation – Stronger Hawaiʻi Fund
donation_links.description: Established disaster relief fund operated by Hawaiʻi Community Foundation. Explicitly recommended by Hawaiʻi Emergency Management Agency (HI-EMA) as an official donation channel for the March 2026 Kona low event. Covers statewide recovery needs.

donation_links.title: Hawaiʻi Foodbank – Flood Relief Monetary and Food Donations
donation_links.description: Hawaiʻi Foodbank is running emergency food distributions for flood-impacted residents on Oʿahu’s North Shore and Central Oʿahu. CEO Amy Miller confirmed expanded operations at March 21 city press conference. Accepts both monetary and food donations. Network of over 250 partner agencies statewide.

donation_links.title: Aloha United Way – Flood Relief Donations
donation_links.description: Aloha United Way is actively operating a help call and text line for flooding victims and accepting monetary donations to support relief efforts on Oʿahu.

donation_links.title: LāHui Foundation – Post-Flood Recovery Efforts 2026
donation_links.description: Local Oʿahu nonprofit mobilizing cleanup crews and coordinating distribution of donated supplies for North Shore flood-impacted communities. Dedicated 2026 flood recovery donation campaign via Donorbox. Also accepting in-kind recovery supplies at Kahuku Elementary School.

donation_links.title: LāHui Foundation – In-Kind Recovery Supply Drive, Kahuku Elementary
donation_links.description: LāHui Foundation accepting in-kind flood recovery supplies at Kahuku Elementary School (56-170 Pualalea St, Kahuku, HI 96731). Recovery items only — no household goods. Hours: 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. Supplies support flood-impacted North Shore families. Reviewer confirmed active. See also cross-seed to help_hubs (donation_dropoff) and volunteer sections.

donation_links.title: Hawaiʻi Farmers Union United – Hawaiʻi Flood Response Fund
donation_links.description: Dedicated flood response fund created by Hawaiʻi Farmers Union United to support farmers and rural communities hardest hit by the Kona low flooding. Accepts monetary donations via PayPal.

donation_links.title: ʻĀina Momona – Loʻi Restoration at Aloha ʻĀina Health and Learning Center, Kāneʻohe
donation_links.description: ʻĀina Momona is restoring flood-damaged loʻi kalo at the Aloha ʻĀina Health and Learning Center in Kāneʻohe. Monetary donations accepted via ActBlue. Represents community-rooted agricultural and cultural restoration work on Windward Oʿahu.

donation_links.title: Hawaiian Council – Kākoʻo Oʿahu: Emergency Relief for Oʿahu Flooding
donation_links.organization: Council for Native Hawaiian Advancement (Hawaiian Council)
donation_links.description: Verified 501(c)(3) public charity (EIN 91-0313383, tax-exempt since July 2003, Charity Navigator 4-star rating). The Hawaiian Council launched Kākoʻo Oʿahu to support individuals and families impacted by flooding on Oʿahu. Funds address housing stability, direct financial assistance, and critical support. Matching every donation dollar-for-dollar up to $100,000. Campaign hosted on Givebutter.

donation_links.title: Convoy of Hope – Oahu, Hawaii Flooding 2026 Response
donation_links.description: National disaster relief nonprofit with a dedicated 2026 Oʿahu flooding response page. Delivering emergency relief supplies to flood-impacted residents on Oʿahu. Donation page is event-specific to the March 2026 flooding.

donation_links.title: GoFundMe – Hawaii Flooding Relief Hub
donation_links.description: GoFundMe centralized Hawaii Flooding Relief hub aggregating verified fundraisers and vetted nonprofits. GoFundMe.org (nonprofit arm) donated $100,000 as a founding grant and is providing direct cash grants to verified flood-impacted individuals and families; grants issued within days of verification. $101,802 raised toward $1M goal as of March 22. Tax-deductible. Quality of individual user campaigns within the broader hub varies — use the GoFundMe.org verified grants pathway for direct aid.

donation_links.title: Maui United Way – Flood Relief Donations
donation_links.description: Maui United Way is collecting monetary donations to support Maui residents affected by the Kona low flooding and other recent disasters.
donation_links.review_reason: Destination URL points to homepage, not a specific flood campaign page. Verify a direct campaign URL exists before publishing.

donation_links.title: American Red Cross – Hawaiʻi and Pacific Islands Disaster Relief
donation_links.description: American Red Cross Pacific Islands chapter is actively supporting sheltering, disaster relief, and recovery operations across all Hawaiian islands during the March 2026 Kona low event. Accepts monetary donations. No ID required at shelters.

donation_links.title: American Red Cross – Volunteer for Hawaiʻi Storm Response
donation_links.description: Red Cross is actively recruiting volunteers to support Hawaiʻi storm response operations including shelter staffing and direct services. Online signup form available.

donation_links.title: LāHui Foundation – Volunteer for Flood Cleanup Crews
donation_links.description: LāHui Foundation is mobilizing volunteer cleanup crews for North Shore Oʿahu flood-impacted communities. Sign up via Google Form.

donation_links.title: Hawaiʻi Foodbank – Volunteer for Food Distribution
donation_links.description: Hawaiʻi Foodbank is recruiting volunteers to support emergency food distribution operations for flood-impacted residents on Oʿahu.

donation_links.title: Farm to Barn – Supply Distribution Hub, Haleʻiwa
donation_links.organization: Farm to Barn / LāHui Foundation / Our Hawaiʻi
donation_links.description: Community supply distribution and staging hub at Farm to Barn in Haleʻiwa. Organized in coordination with LāHui Foundation and Our Hawaiʻi with support from Councilmember Weyer and Rep. Quinlan. Distributing bottled water, shelf-stable food, cleaning supplies, mops, buckets, and towels for residents of Waialua, Mokulēʻia, and Haleʻiwa impacted by the Kona low storms. March 22 hours 10 a.m.–4 p.m.; confirm ongoing availability.
donation_links.hours: Sun Mar 22: 10:00 a.m. – 4:00 p.m.; confirm ongoing schedule

donation_links.title: Old Waialua Sugar Mill – Community Staging Hub and Water Distribution
donation_links.organization: Waialua Community / LāHui Foundation / Our Hawaiʻi
donation_links.description: Community staging hub for ongoing North Shore flood recovery, coordinated with LāHui Foundation, Our Hawaiʻi, Councilmember Weyer, and Rep. Quinlan. Clean water buffalo (potable drinking water tanker) confirmed on site March 21 for residents under active boil water notice. Staging hub role continues through recovery period for Waialua, Mokulēʻia, and Haleʻiwa. Accepting donated supplies; confirm what is needed before traveling.
donation_links.hours: Water buffalo: Mar 21 evening. Supply staging: ongoing through recovery; confirm hours

donation_links.title: Clean Water Tanker – Waialua Community Association
donation_links.description: Clean water tanker stationed at Waialua Community Association for North Shore residents under the active boil water notice. For residents unable to boil water. Also confirmed at Banzai Skatepark and Waialua District Park Recreation Center. Active while boil water notice remains in effect.
donation_links.source_name: Aloha State Daily / Hawaiʻi Public Radio
donation_links.hours: Active while boil water notice is in effect; confirm duration

review.reason: Destination URL points to homepage, not a specific flood campaign page. Verify a direct campaign URL exists before publishing.
review.recommended_next_step: Visit mauiunitedway.org, find specific flood campaign page, update destination_url.

review.reason: Cross-seed advisory: this in-kind record should also be seeded to help_hubs (resource_type=donation_dropoff, area=North Shore, neighborhood=Kahuku) and linked from the volunteer section since LāHui is concurrently accepting cleanup volunteers.
review.recommended_next_step: Run help_hubs upsert with external_id='hub-lahui-foundation-inkind-kahuku', resource_type='donation_dropoff', address='56-170 Pualalea St, Kahuku, HI 96731'. Also link vol-lahui-foundation-cleanup-crews from the same org card.

notes: PATCH run: 19 active records (18 is_visible=true, 1 is_visible=false). 5 records archived (not deleted).
donation_type distribution: community_campaign=4, in_kind_support=4, institutional=7, platform_hub=1, volunteer=3
SALVATION ARMY: Campaign conflict resolved. don-salvation-army-hawaii-kona-low-2026 (campaign 779616) confirmed canonical. don-salvation-army-hawaii-general-disaster-2026 archived.

GOFUNDME: URL corrected to https://www.gofundme.com/communities/hawaii-flooding. don-gofundme-maui-2026-flood-relief-fund merged into statewide hub record (GoFundMe.org founding grant, direct cash grant pathway, and $101,802 raised detail absorbed into description). Maui record archived.

HAWAIIAN COUNCIL / GIVEBUTTER: don-givebutter-north-shore-community and don-hawaiian-council-kakoo-oahu confirmed as the same campaign. Merged under don-hawaiian-council-kakoo-oahu with Givebutter URL. Organization verified: Council for Native Hawaiian Advancement, EIN 91-0313383, 501(c)(3) since July 2003, Charity Navigator 4-star. Badges updated. Givebutter record archived.

LAHUI IN-KIND: don-lahui-foundation-inkind-supplies made visible (reviewer approved). Cross-seed advisory added to review queue for help_hubs (donation_dropoff) and volunteer section. inkind-lahui-foundation-kahuku-elementary (P2B duplicate) archived in its favor.

INKIND EVENTS: inkind-waialua-ucc-supplies removed (user request). Farm to Barn and Old Waialua Sugar Mill updated with Instagram post context (https://www.instagram.com/p/DWLEPQ3DSX0/): organization names, supply types, staging hub role, and water buffalo detail added. Haleʻiwa neighborhood confirmed for Farm to Barn.

SCHEMA MIGRATION: neighborhood, address, hours added as nullable TEXT columns. schema_migration SQL key provided separately from upsert. Run migration before upsert. 5 records carry physical location data in these new fields.

ARCHIVE APPROACH: Archived records are soft-deleted via UPDATE SET is_visible=false rather than DELETE. Retained for audit trail. archive_sql key provided. A status or archived_at column would improve this pattern — recommend adding in next migration.
don-maui-united-way-flood-relief remains needs_review=true (homepage URL, no specific campaign page found).

badge enum candidates now include: 'Established Organization', 'Local Organization', 'Newly Added', 'Platform Verified', 'Limited Verification', '501(c)(3) Verified', 'Charity Navigator 4-Star', 'Matching Fund'. Harden into migration before next seed run.
donation_type enum candidates: institutional, community_campaign, in_kind_support, platform_hub, volunteer.
All SQL uses ON CONFLICT (external_id) DO UPDATE — idempotent upsert safe to re-run.
Unicode Hawaiian diacriticals (ʻokina, macrons) preserved as UTF-8 throughout.
